data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <bead id="ddx-669e1c2f">
    <title>runs cross-links + e2e: bead↔run, run↔artifact, runs.spec.ts</title>
    <description>
Wire up cross-navigation links and write the runs.spec.ts e2e test.

Cross-links:
- Bead detail: linked run records (layer=try or run) with status, click → runs detail
- Run detail (work/try): beadId links to bead detail (/beads/:beadId)
- Run detail (run layer): if artifactId present, shows 'Produced artifact: <title>' link to /artifacts/:artifactId
- Artifact detail: if producing run exists, 'View producing run' link is live (navigates to run detail)
- Combined /nodes/:nodeId/runs: row links navigate to correct project-scoped /runs/:runId (carrying projectId from row data)

E2E (runs.spec.ts):
Full US-086b + US-094b workflow:
1. Navigate to project runs
2. Apply layer=work filter → verify URL + filtered list
3. Click work record → verify work detail fields
4. Click child try → verify try fields (bead link, revisions, worktreePath, mergeOutcome)
5. Click child run → verify run fields (power, harness, tokens, cost, evidence)
6. Follow evidence link → verify artifact link present
7. Navigate breadcrumbs back up: run → try → work → list
8. Verify filter state restored after navigating back

Depends on ddx-a9c6ad1c (runs detail), ddx-43684ac1 (artifact provenance for backlink).
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. Bead detail shows linked run records; clicking navigates to run detail.
2. Run try/work detail: beadId links navigate to bead detail; Back returns to run detail.
3. Run-layer detail: artifact link present when artifactId set; link is live.
4. Artifact detail: 'View producing run' link is live and navigates to run detail.
5. Combined runs list: row click carries correct projectId in URL.
6. runs.spec.ts passes: complete work→try→run drill-down, breadcrumb back-navigation, artifact link.
    </acceptance>
    <notes>
REVIEW:BLOCK

Diff only contains execution evidence files (manifest.json, result.json) under .ddx/executions/. No actual implementation changes (cross-link UI code, runs.spec.ts e2e test) are visible in the provided diff, so none of the 6 AC items can be verified.
    </notes>
    <labels>area:ui, area:frontend, feat-008, feat-021, runs</labels>
    <metadata spec-id="FEAT-021" base-rev="c529d15307bb7985176b936566a5a7e8f7d27c9f" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260501T072937-a96ae1b7"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-021" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-021-dashboard-ui.md">Feature: Multi-Node Dashboard UI</ref>
  </governing>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> sections above are the completion contract — every AC checkbox must be provably satisfied by a specific piece of code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## How to work

1. Read first. If the bead description names files, read them to see what is already there. If the bead references a spec, ADR, or API contract, read the relevant sections before writing code. Do not start editing until you understand what the bead wants changed and why.

2. Cross-reference your work against the acceptance criteria as you go. Before committing, walk through the AC one item at a time and identify the specific test name, file path, or function that satisfies each checkbox. If you cannot point to concrete evidence for an AC item, it is not done.

3. Run the project's verification commands before committing. Use whatever commands match the crate or package you touched — typically `cargo test` and `cargo clippy` for Rust, `bun test` or `npm test` for JS/TS, `go test` for Go. The bead description usually names the exact commands. **Do not commit red code.** If a test or lint fails, fix it first.

4. Commit once, when everything is green. Stage only the files you intentionally changed with `git add <specific-paths>` — never `git add -A`, because there may be unrelated WIP in this worktree. Use a conventional-commit-style subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. DDx will merge your commits back to the base branch.

5. If you cannot complete the bead in this pass, writing `.ddx/executions/20260501T072937-a96ae1b7/no_changes_rationale.txt` is mandatory before you exit. Include: (a) what is done, (b) what is blocking, (c) what a follow-up attempt would need. If you exit without a commit and without this file, DDx records `no_evidence_produced` and treats the attempt as a harness failure, not as legitimate no_changes. Do NOT commit partial, exploratory, or red code hoping the reviewer will accept it — a well-justified no_changes is better than a bad commit. The bead will be re-queued for another attempt, potentially with a stronger model.

## The bead contract overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override any CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or project-level conservative defaults in this worktree. If the bead asks for new documentation, write it. If the bead adds a new module or crate, add it. Conservative rules (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) do not apply inside execute-bead — the bead IS the ask.

## Quality bar and the review step

After your commit merges, an automated review step may check your work against the acceptance criteria. If any AC item is unmet, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher-capability model, and the review findings are threaded into the next attempt's prompt as a `<review-findings>` section. **The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet the AC in this pass so the bead closes cleanly.**

If this prompt already contains a `<review-findings>` section, address every BLOCKING finding before claiming the work complete. Those findings are the precise list of what the previous attempt missed.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is already initialized. Running it corrupts the bead queue and project configuration.
- Do not modify files outside the scope the bead description names.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or any other project-instructions file unless the bead explicitly asks for it.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and you have verified every AC item, stop. Return control to the orchestrator. Do not continue to explore the repository, run extra tests, or generate follow-up notes — the bead is complete, and returning promptly is how execute-bead signals success.
  </instructions>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
All 6 acceptance criteria for ddx-669e1c2f are already satisfied by commit `44c5014a` (an ancestor of the current base `c529d153`). The prior REVIEW:BLOCK was a misread of an evidence-only diff — the actual implementation (BeadDetail linked-runs section, run-detail bead/artifact links, artifact-detail producing-run link, combined `/nodes/:nodeId/runs` projectId-aware row navigation, and `runs.spec.ts`) is present on main.

Wrote `.ddx/executions/20260501T072937-a96ae1b7/no_changes_rationale.txt` mapping each AC item to the file/line that satisfies it. No commit produced.
